title: fix(image): pass seed to vertex ai models
author: fisventurous

gist.headline: Planting new seeds
gist.blurb: Fixed a quirky bug where our nanobanana image models were getting a little too attached to their first drafts. Now, passing a new seed guarantees a fresh, unique variation every single time.
gist.summary: Passed the seed parameter to Vertex AI requests for nanobanana models to ensure distinct image variations on each generation.
gist.impact: Users will now receive properly varied images when regenerating or editing with nanobanana models, rather than identical cached results.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about 'seeds' and 'variations' in image generation. Depict Polly the bee planting glowing digital seeds in a garden, with each seed sprouting a completely different, unique pixel-art flower or glowing image frame to represent the fixed variation generation.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot planting glowing lime green (#ecf874) seeds in a digital garden, with each seed sprouting a completely unique, colorful pixel flower. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head watches happily, holding a small watering can. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites.
